timestamp_ns.description: Event timestamp in nanoseconds since Unix epoch

item_type.description: BTM launch-item type (es_btm_item_type_t). 'daemon' = system-domain LaunchDaemon (T1543.004), 'agent' = LaunchAgent, 'login_item' = login item. 'unknown' is the extension's fallback for a future es_btm_item_type_t case it does not yet map.

item_path.description: Filesystem path of the launch item (the plist), from es_btm_launch_item_t.item_url.

executable_path.description: Optional. POSIX executable path from the launchd plist, when available.

executable_code_signing.description: Optional. Code-signing of the registered executable (executable_path), evaluated via SecStaticCode by the agent (the extension's sandbox cannot read the registered executable on a SIP-enabled host, so the agent fills this field before upload). This is the rule's decision input (the instigator is Apple's smd for launchctl-bootstrap registrations and cannot discriminate).

legacy.description: True iff a legacy plist-based launch item rather than an SMAppService registration.

managed.description: True iff managed by MDM. Managed daemons are operator-deployed and skipped by detection rules.

uid.description: User ID the item is registered for.

instigator_pid.description: Optional. PID of the process that registered the item (es_event_btm_launch_item_add_t.instigator). Forensic context only: for a launchctl-bootstrap registration the instigator is Apple's smd.

pidversion.description: Optional. Kernel PID generation (audit_token_to_pidversion) of the exec'd process, read from its audit token. Lets the server correlate flows to the exact process generation independently of PID reuse. Absent on agents that predate this field.

cdhash.description: Code-directory hash (sha1 of the CDDirectory blob) the agent extracts on Hardened-Runtime binaries (issue #68). Optional: NULL for non-HR processes since the ESF target tuple lacks a meaningful cdhash for them. 40 lowercase hex characters when present.

snapshot.description: True for synthetic exec events emitted at extension startup to materialise processes that existed before ESF subscription (issue #11). Detection rules skip snapshot events because they describe historical state, not new attacker activity.

pidversion.description: Optional. Kernel PID generation (audit_token_to_pidversion) of the CHILD process, read from its audit token. Stored on the new process generation so flows can correlate by exact identity. Absent on agents that predate this field.

exit_reason.description: Optional. 'host_reconciled' tags exits synthesized by the agent's kill(pid,0) reconciliation pass when the kernel-observed exit event went missing (issue #6 client half). Absent or 'event' means the kernel observed the exit.

pidversion.description: Optional. Kernel PID generation (audit_token_to_pidversion) of the source process, read from the flow's audit token. Lets the server correlate the flow to the exact process generation independently of PID reuse. Absent on agents that predate this field or when the flow carried no usable audit token.

coalesced_count.description: Optional. Number of occurrences this representative event stands for after agent-side coalescing (issue #408). The agent collapses repeated identical 5-tuples (pid, pidversion, protocol, direction, remote_address, remote_port) seen within the agent's coalescing window into one event. Absent or 1 means a single occurrence. The envelope timestamp_ns is the earliest occurrence.

last_timestamp_ns.description: Optional. Timestamp (ns since Unix epoch) of the latest occurrence in a coalesced series; the envelope timestamp_ns is the earliest. Present only when coalesced_count is greater than 1. Lets a consumer recover the span of the coalesced window.

response_addresses.description: Resolved addresses. After agent-side coalescing (issue #408) this is the union of every address observed across the merged queries for the same (pid, pidversion, query_name, query_type) within the agent's coalescing window, so a later connection to any resolved address still correlates.

pidversion.description: Optional. Kernel PID generation (audit_token_to_pidversion) of the querying process, read from the flow's audit token. Lets the server correlate the query to the exact process generation independently of PID reuse. Absent on agents that predate this field or when the flow carried no usable audit token.

coalesced_count.description: Optional. Number of occurrences this representative event stands for after agent-side coalescing (issue #408). The agent collapses repeated lookups for the same (pid, pidversion, query_name, query_type) seen within the agent's coalescing window into one event, merging a query with its follow-on response. Absent or 1 means a single occurrence. The envelope timestamp_ns is the earliest occurrence.

last_timestamp_ns.description: Optional. Timestamp (ns since Unix epoch) of the latest occurrence in a coalesced series; the envelope timestamp_ns is the earliest. Present only when coalesced_count is greater than 1.

snapshot_heartbeat_payload.description: Periodic liveness signal emitted by the agent for PIDs that were materialised via the issue #11 ESF startup snapshot. The server's graph builder updates processes.last_seen_ns on receipt; the TTL reconciler reads COALESCE(last_seen_ns, fork_time_ns) so a snapshot row with fresh heartbeats is exempt from the 6h force-exit (issue #173).

rule_id.description: Stable string identifier of the matched application-control rule (e.g. "app_control:42"). Echoed by the server as the alert's rule_id.

identifier.description: The matched identifier value (e.g. the SHA-256 the BINARY rule matched against).

application_control_undecided_payload.description: Emitted when AUTH_EXEC could not compute a BINARY hash within the kernel deadline budget (or the file was unreadable for the TOCTOU re-stat reasons in FileHashCache.computeSHA256WithDeadline) and the active snapshot's deadline_fallback drove the verdict. Operators consume these events to size their cold-cache rate before flipping the posture between fail-closed / fail-open / audit-only.

verdict.description: Wire verdict the kernel received. "deny" only fires under fail-closed posture; "allow" fires under audit-only (fail-open emits no event by design).

reason.description: Technical cause that prevented a hash decision. "deadline" means the budget headroom was exhausted before chunks finished streaming; "read_failed" means the file could not be opened, fstat failed, or a TOCTOU replace was detected between AUTH and read.

file_size_bytes.description: Size of the executable being authorised. Pairs with reason="deadline" to identify large-binary workloads where the deadline budget is the bottleneck.

application_control_resync_payload.description: Emitted when the extension accepts an application-control snapshot whose policy_version regressed below the active snapshot's but whose policy_epoch (the policy's server-assigned updated_at in microseconds) advanced. That pairing is the signature of a server database restore-from-backup or reset: policy_version restarts low while the host retains a higher persisted version, so the version-only gate would freeze enforcement. The extension re-syncs on the epoch axis and emits this event so the regression is operator-visible instead of being a host-only log line (#322).

previous_version.description: The policy_version the host held before accepting the regressed snapshot.

new_version.description: The lower policy_version the host re-synced to.

previous_epoch.description: The policy_epoch (updated_at microseconds) the host held before the re-sync.

new_epoch.description: The advanced policy_epoch that drove acceptance despite the version regression.

reason.description: Why the re-sync fired. "version_regression" is the only cause today: a lower version accepted because the epoch advanced.
